skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Video outpainting on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI — extend the spatial canvas of a video, change aspect ratio (9:16 vertical to 16:9 horizontal or vice versa), add environment beyond the original frame while preserving the central action.
skill.summary: Extend video canvas spatially — uncrop, change aspect ratio, or add environment using AI-powered outpainting.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: The skill is a pure markdown documentation file with no executable code. All 33 'external_commands' findings are false positives — the backticks detected are markdown formatting and bash code block examples, not actual command execution. The skill explicitly scopes bash usage to 'Bash(runcomfy *)' only. All 22 network URLs are legitimate documentation and marketing links to runcomfy.com and skills.sh. The skill includes a thorough Security & Privacy section documenting token storage, input boundaries, and outbound endpoint allowlists. No prompt injection attempts were detected.

low_findings.title: External command references in documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 33 backtick patterns as potential shell execution. Upon review, all are markdown formatting or bash code block examples demonstrating runcomfy CLI usage. The skill frontmatter restricts bash to 'Bash(runcomfy *)' only, limiting actual execution surface.

low_findings.title: Network endpoints referenced in documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 22 hardcoded URLs. All are legitimate documentation and marketing links to runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, and skills.sh. The skill explicitly states outbound endpoints are limited to model-api.runcomfy.net and *.runcomfy.net/*.runcomfy.com.

low_findings.title: Token storage in home directory config
low_findings.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login stores API token at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is a standard configuration file location. The skill also recommends using RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable in CI/container environments.

content.user_title: Extend Video Canvas with AI Outpainting
content.value_statement: Videos with wrong aspect ratios get rejected by platforms or look unprofessional. This skill uses AI to spatially extend video frames, converting vertical clips to horizontal or adding environment beyond original frames.
actual_capabilities: Extend video spatial canvas using Wan 2-7 edit-video model
Change aspect ratio from 9:16 vertical to 16:9 horizontal
Convert 16:9 horizontal content to 9:16 vertical format
Add environment beyond original frame while preserving central action
Route to ComfyUI outpainting workflows for hero-grade seam quality
Process videos via runcomfy CLI with JSON-based input parameters
limitations: Quality variance at seams — Wan 2-7 was not trained specifically for outpainting
Quality degrades after 2 passes of multi-step outpainting
Image outpainting requires the separate image-outpainting skill
Temporal video extension (more frames) requires the video-extend skill

use_cases.title: Social media content repurposing
use_cases.description: Convert TikTok vertical videos to YouTube horizontal format for cross-platform publishing.

use_cases.title: Brand asset adaptation
use_cases.description: Resize square Instagram content to wide banner formats while preserving subject framing.

use_cases.title: Archive footage modernization
use_cases.description: Convert old 4:3 footage to modern 16:9 aspect ratio using ComfyUI workflows for careful seam handling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic aspect ratio swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint this vertical video to 16:9 horizontal. Use Wan 2-7 edit-video with prompt: 'Extend the canvas to 16:9 horizontal by adding matching environment on the left and right sides.'
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner — simple vertical to horizontal conversion

prompt_templates.title: Social media optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this TikTok video to YouTube format. Run: runcomfy run wan-ai/wan-2-7/edit-video with prompt emphasizing matching background style and lighting preservation.

prompt_templates.title: Hero-grade outpainting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ComfyUI LTX 2-3 outpainting workflow for this video. This route handles temporal consistency and seam quality for hero ad delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-pass extension
prompt_templates.prompt: For extensive canvas extension: Pass 1 extends ~30% with Wan 2-7, then re-pass on the output. Limit to 2 passes to avoid quality degradation.

output_examples.input: User: Convert my vertical TikTok video to YouTube horizontal format
output_examples.output: The skill invokes runcomfy run wan-ai/wan-2-7/edit-video with an aspect-ratio swap prompt. The CLI sends the video to model-api.runcomfy.net, processes it, and downloads the 16:9 result to the specified output directory.

output_examples.input: User: I need hero-quality outpainting for an ad campaign
output_examples.output: The skill recommends the ComfyUI LTX 2-3 outpainting workflow for temporal consistency and seam quality. Since this is a GUI workflow, the agent directs the user to open it in RunComfy ComfyUI cloud rather than the CLI.

output_examples.input: User: Extend my square Instagram video to a wide banner
output_examples.output: The skill uses Route 1 with a prompt that extends both sides while preserving the central subject. Output is a 16:9 video ready for banner placement.

best_practices: Always include preservation language in prompts: 'Preserve the original action and subject framing in the center'
Describe what extends clearly: same background style, lighting, depth of field, and camera distance
Use ComfyUI workflows when seam quality is critical for hero delivery or ad campaigns
anti_patterns: Do not use Wan 2-7 for hero ad delivery where strict seam-quality requirements exist — use ComfyUI workflows instead
Do not exceed 2 passes of multi-step outpainting — quality degrades significantly after that
Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell — install only via npm or npx

faq.question: What aspect ratio conversions are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports converting between 9:16 vertical and 16:9 horizontal, square to wide formats, and 4:3 to 16:9 for archive footage modernization.

faq.question: When should I use ComfyUI workflows instead of the CLI?
faq.answer: Use ComfyUI workflows when you need hero-grade seam quality for ad delivery, strict temporal consistency, or motion-aware fill. The CLI Wan 2-7 route is suitable for social media and internal use.

faq.question: How does the skill handle API token security?
faq.answer: The runcomfy CLI stores tokens in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. For CI or container environments, use the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: What are the outbound network endpoints?
faq.answer: The skill only connects to model-api.runcomfy.net and *.runcomfy.net/*.runcomfy.com domains. No telemetry is sent to other endpoints.

faq.question: Can I outpaint images with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically for video outpainting. For single image outpainting, use the image-outpainting skill instead.

faq.question: What is the maximum file size for downloads?
faq.answer: The CLI aborts any single download larger than 2 GiB to prevent resource exhaustion.
